title: MySQL Root Account Without Password
description: MySQL 5.0.51a is accessible remotely with the root account requiring no password. The root user has full administrative privileges over all databases. A guest account also exists with remote access (host: %). This allows complete database compromise including data theft, modification, and potential OS command execution.
evidence:
Exploitation command:
$ mysql -h 192.168.0.140 -u root --skip-password --skip-ssl -e "SELECT user,host FROM mysql.user; SELECT version();"

Output:
user             host
debian-sys-maint 
guest            %
root             %

version()
5.0.51a-3ubuntu5

Root access confirmed with no password. Guest account also available remotely.
remediation: Set a strong password for the MySQL root account. Remove the guest account. Restrict remote access by binding MySQL to localhost or specific trusted IPs. Remove unnecessary user accounts.

suggestions: For SQLi: Show extracted data or UNION-based output
Include sqlmap output showing database/table extraction
